findings.raw_text: Creatinine: 1.3 mg/dL (above baseline of 1.1 - mild acute on chronic)
Lactate: 2.4 mmol/L (ELEVATED - tissue hypoperfusion, concern for sepsis)
Procalcitonin: 18.4 ng/mL (markedly elevated - bacterial infection strongly suggested)
decreased urine output noted by wife
mild shortness of breath on exertion
Urine output critically low: 15 mL/hr over the past 8 hours
Mental status significantly declined: GCS now 12, previously 14 on admission
New mottling bilateral lower extremities - peripheral hypoperfusion
WBC: 18.7 K/uL (HIGH - was 12.2 on admission. Rising trend = worsening infection)
Lactate: 3.8 mmol/L (CRITICAL - was 2.4 yesterday. Clearance not achieved. Target <2.0)
Creatinine: 1.6 mg/dL (above baseline 1.1. Rising - AKI Stage 1 by KDIGO: 1.6/1.1 = 1.45x)
Platelets: 148 K/uL (down from 210 on admission - concerning trend)
Still disoriented - does not know date or location
Mottling on bilateral lower extremities slightly improved from morning
BP more stable overnight ranging 104-112/66-72 mmHg
Mental status marginally improving - wife notes brief lucid moments
Urine output last 12 hours (1800-0600): 280 mL = 23 mL/hr
WBC flagged at 0200 draw: value 1.8 K/uL returned from lab
Mental status continues to improve dramatically
Lactate: 1.4 mmol/L (NORMAL - down from 3.8 at peak
ACUTE KIDNEY INJURY: Creatinine 1.7, stable to improving
MENTAL STATUS: Returned to baseline GCS 15
Patient noticeably improved from this morning. Oriented x3 - knows name, hospital, and approximate date
Patient reports dyspnea much improved - 'I can breathe better now.'
Patient ate approximately half of dinner - significant improvement in appetite

rag_guidelines_output.query_used: Symptoms: hypotension (moderate; BP nadir 94/60 mmHg at 0230), altered mental status (moderate; Mental status significantly declined: GCS now 12, previously 14 on admission), peripheral hypoperfusion (moderate; New mottling bilateral lower extremities - peripheral hypoperfusion), hypoxia (moderate; SpO2: 94% on 4L NC (worsening hypoxia)), fever (moderate; Temp: 38.7C (persistent fever)), tachycardia (moderate; HR: 104 bpm (tachycardia persistent)), tachypnea (moderate; RR: 22 breaths/min (tachypnea)), leukocytosis (severe; WBC: 18.7 K/uL (HIGH - was 12.2 on admission. Rising trend = worsening infection)), acute kidney injury (moderate; Creatinine: 1.6 mg/dL (above baseline 1.1. Rising - AKI Stage 1 by KDIGO: 1.6/1.1 = 1.45x)), thrombocytopenia (moderate; Platelets: 148 K/uL (down from 210 on admission - concerning trend)), sepsis (moderate; SEPSIS: Clinically RESOLVING), acute kidney injury (moderate; ACUTE KIDNEY INJURY: Creatinine 1.7, stable to improving), pneumonia (moderate; PNEUMONIA: Right lower/middle lobe consolidation), neurological response (moderate; Marginal improvement noted since morning), disorientation (moderate; Still disoriented - does not know date or location), glasgow coma scale (moderate; GCS 13 (E3V4M6)), crackles (moderate; Crackles right lower zone persist), sputum (moderate; Sputum: thick, yellow-green tinged), urine output (severe; Urine output 0600-1400 (8 hours): 185 mL = 23 mL/hr), fever (moderate; TEMPERATURE: 38.5C at 1400), mottling (moderate; Mottling on bilateral lower extremities slightly improved from morning), fall risk (severe; Fall risk score HIGH), heart rate (moderate; HR 102 bpm), tachycardia (moderate; Tachycardia persisting HR 98-108), mental status (moderate; Mental status marginally improving - wife notes brief lucid moments), glasgow coma scale (moderate; GCS 13 (E4V4M5)), temperature (moderate; Temperature: 38.2C at 0545), urine output (moderate; Urine output last 12 hours (1800-0600): 280 mL = 23 mL/hr), norepinephrine (moderate; Norepinephrine 0.06 mcg/kg/min continuous), mental status (moderate; Mental status continues to improve dramatically), wbc (moderate; WBC: 15.8 K/uL (DOWN from 17.2), creatinine (moderate; Creatinine: 1.7 mg/dL (stable from 1.8 yesterday). Abnormal labs: Creatinine 1.66 elevated, Lactate 2.49 elevated, WBC 13.57 elevated. Retrieve the most relevant ICU clinical guideline evidence, thresholds, and diagnostic criteria for the flagged risks.

citations.rationale: SEPSIS_RISK matched: hypotension, altered mental status, oliguria, lactate > 2 mmol/L, WBC > 12 K/uL, creatinine elevated. Guideline from Sepsis-3 Consensus Definitions supports this risk. Evidence: SECTION 1 Sepsis-3 Definitions and Diagnostic Criteria Source: Singer M, et al. The Third International Consensus Definitions for Sepsis and Septic Shock (Sepsis-3). JAMA. 2016;315
citations.threshold: Sepsis-3: suspected infection plus acute SOFA increase >= 2

citations.rationale: SEPTIC_SHOCK_RISK matched: persistent hypotension, lactate > 2 mmol/L. Guideline from Septic Shock Identification and Management supports this risk. Evidence: Septic shock requires sepsis plus persistent hypotension needing vasopressors to maintain MAP >=65 mmHg and lactate >2 mmol/L despite adequate fluid resuscitation.
citations.threshold: Septic shock: vasopressor-dependent MAP >= 65 with lactate > 2 mmol/L after fluids

citations.rationale: AKI_RISK matched: oliguria, creatinine elevated. Guideline from KDIGO AKI Guidelines 2012 supports this risk. Evidence: KDIGO AKI definition: creatinine rise >=0.3 mg/dL in 48 hours, or >=1.5x baseline in 7 days, or urine output <0.5 mL/kg/hour for >=6 hours.
citations.threshold: KDIGO AKI: creatinine rise >= 0.3 mg/dL in 48h or urine output < 0.5 mL/kg/hr for >= 6h

citations.rationale: EARLY_WARNING_ESCALATION matched: hypotension, altered mental status, tachycardia. Guideline from NEWS2 Early Warning Score supports this risk. Evidence: SECTION 9 National Early Warning Score 2 (NEWS2) Source: Royal College of Physicians. National Early Warning Score (NEWS) 2. Updated report of a working party. London: RCP, 2017. 9
citations.threshold: NEWS2 high risk: score >= 7 or any single severe parameter
